title: orobanche epithymum var. hellebori
intro: Orobanche epithymum var. hellebori, also known as Hellebore Broomrape, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of Helleborus species. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving spasms, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Orobanche epithymum var. hellebori can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
